mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates above 4.5% compress equity discount rates and increase corporate borrowing costs, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal triggers but 5-day forward returns are positive in >70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and real sales of new houses signal a broad contraction in residential demand that spills over into consumer durables and financial sector earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day returns (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects robust manufacturing and utility output that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in >60% of observations during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation above 0.35% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, forcing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above threshold but 5-day returns remain positive in >65% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive payroll/negative unemployment signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects sustained aggregate demand that translates into higher corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sudden supply-side contraction or demand shock that triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Threshold breach occurs but 5-day forward returns are positive in >70% of triggered instances.
